strengths: Demonstrated impact via dashboards and automation
Lending and credit risk analytical project experience
weaknesses: No documented contract negotiation or pricing work
Lacks stated FCRA/GLBA or regulatory experience
No real-time data integration or low-latency SLAs shown
Minimal people management tenure and senior leadership exposure
improvement_bullets: Evaluated third-party data providers for loan portfolio risk, conducting data profiling and quality assessments to ensure alignment with FCRA/GLBA compliance standards and project objectives.
Partnered with cross-functional leadership to support vendor contract negotiations and scenario modeling, translating data acquisition needs into actionable financial forecasts and ROI requirements.
Led end-to-end data integration projects by architecting real-time data pipelines and batch processes, ensuring strict adherence to latency SLAs and data reliability metrics.
Mentored junior analysts on experiment design and data storytelling, championing best practices for building decision algorithms and performance tracking frameworks.
Designed and executed A/B testing frameworks in production environments to validate the predictive lift of new data elements on customer acquisition and model performance.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Sr. Manager, Strategic Third-Party Data Acquisition opportunity with Gen. I am interested in this opportunity because of Gen’s commitment to providing global digital freedom, and I am drawn to your data-driven approach to cyber safety and financial wellness. I believe my expertise in data-driven decisioning and my technical foundation in Python, SQL, and predictive modeling would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached complex data problems with a strategic, business-first mindset. In fact, at Shine Masters, I identified that our operational bottlenecks were caused by fragmented data, so I led the design of an automated ETL pipeline and Power BI integration. This shift not only reduced data processing time by 40% but also provided the high-level visibility needed to increase customer acquisition by 300%. Ultimately, I know that the Sr. Manager, Strategic Third-Party Data Acquisition role at Gen would allow me to bring my experience in data quality and value assessment to your strategic data acquisition roadmap. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst & Operations Lead enabled me to develop my vendor and project management skills. For example, when streamlining our intake workflows using Zapier, I evaluated multiple third-party integration vendors, negotiated service capabilities, and successfully implemented a solution that saved 8 hours per week while ensuring data consistency. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in data modeling and experimentation in working on third-party vendor relationships and real-time data integration at Gen. I know that my proficiency in building end-to-end data solutions and my passion for actionable analytics align with what it takes to succeed at the Sr. Manager, Strategic Third-Party Data Acquisition role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
